label: DIFFERENT
rationale: NEW is a specific datum (control diet ~28–29% sucrose by calories); CAND is the general principle that CR benefit depends on baseline diet quality. Concrete evidentiary figure vs general principle.

label: SAME
rationale: SAME recommendation (refeed carbs before OGTT in low-carb-adapted people); CAND's "~3 days" is added precision, no conflict.

label: DIFFERENT
rationale: NEW carries specific effect sizes (hazard ratio ≈5 bottom-25th vs top-2%; ≈2.75 vs 50–75th); CAND is the general "VO2max is the strongest predictor of mortality". Specific magnitudes vs general association.

label: DIFFERENT
rationale: Different interval prescriptions: NEW = 3–4 min intervals gauged by recovery time (<4 min too easy, ~10 min too hard); CAND = 3–8 min near-max at ~1:1 work:rest. Distinct actionable content.

label: DIFFERENT
rationale: Different claims: NEW says elite VO2max (80s–90s) is unnecessary; CAND says benefit concentrates in the top ~2.5% (~52 ml/kg/min for a 50-yr-old). A ceiling claim vs a target with a different number.

label: SAME
rationale: SAME "sperm is a tiny, highly specialised cell" claim; matching dimensions (few-µm head, 35-µm tail). NEW's ~300 motility genes is elaboration of the same claim. BORDERLINE.

label: DIFFERENT
rationale: Different timeframe for epididymal maturation: NEW ~2 weeks (35-foot tubule); CAND ~10 days with ~1-in-4 transit attrition. Material timeframe/quantity difference.

label: SAME

label: DIFFERENT
rationale: NEW gives a quantitative cause breakdown (varicocele ~40%, hormonal ~10–15%, genetics less modifiable); CAND only states varicocele is the most common modifiable cause. NEW bundles specific proportions across multiple causes the general ranking lacks.
